title: Cantinoa multiseta
taxonomy: Cantinoa multiseta is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is native to South America and belongs to the genus Cantinoa. Cantinoa multiseta is closely related to other members of the Lamiaceae family, which includes herbs and shrubs. The classification of Cantinoa multiseta places it within the broader group of Lamiaceae, a family of plants commonly known as mint or deadnettle. Further classification may also include the order Lamiales, which encompasses a wide range of flowering plants.

intro: Cantinoa multiseta, also known as Mariana's Candytuft, is a plant species belonging to the family Plantaginaceae, native to the high-altitude regions of the Andes. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Cantinoa multiseta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Cantinoa multiseta is commonly prepared as an herbal tea or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and gastrointestinal problems.
